videos.transcript: I am at a place which is not only popular in India, but across the globe. Foreigners know more about this place than Indians. I am at Agra, which is home to one of the 7 wonders of the World. Yes, the Taj Mahal is located in Agra, Uttar Pradesh. Everyone knows about the Taj Mahal, there are many other places to explore too, which have great cultural and historical importance. If you come here do visit these places. I am Dr. Anuj Bansal and welcome to Anuj Bucket. Today, we will be exploring the top attractions of Agra. There are many places that you can explore in Agra other than the Taj Mahal. What are those places and all their information will be covered in this video. As you can see the Agra Fort behind me. Do watch the video till the end, and subscribe to the channel. Subscribe to the Channel and press the bell icon. Find the exact location of all these places in our description box. Click the Google maps link in the description. 1. Taj Mahal. This is the most famous landmark of Agra. The Taj Mahal is considered symbol of Love, It was made by Shah Jahan for Mumtaz Ji. It is named after Mumtaz, made up of white Marble, it is part of a UNESCO Heritage Site. It is also included in the 7 Wonders of the World. It is not only popular in India but all over the World. It took 20 years to complete the construction It is also said that Shah Jahan had ordered to cut down hands of the workers so no one would replicate this wonder. The Workers were called from Persia and Italy. They deserve all the credit to make the World's most beautiful wonder. Every couple wishes to visit this symbol of Love. Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan made this in the loving memory of Mumtaz Begum. You can see their graves in this place. That part of the Taj Mahal is restricted to normal public. The real beauty of the Taj Mahal can be found in full moonlight. The ticket prices are 50₹ for Indians and 1100₹ for Foreigners. You need to pay 250₹ more to visit the Makbara. It is open from 6AM-6.30 PM. 2. Agra Fort. Our Next place is Agra Fort also known as Laal Kila If you like Historical monuments do not miss out on coming here. Very few people know that this Red Fort was constructed first. Diwan-i-Aam and Khaas Mahal are famous attractions of the Agra Fort. Other attractions are Sheesh Mahal and Jahangir Palace. After looking at Agra Fort, you will find out that there are other places to explore than the Taj Mahal in Agra. You will understand the grandeur of Mughals after seeing this Fort. This Tourist attraction has many attractions inside. The Diwan-i-Aam was used by the King to listen the problem of Locals. Nagina Masjid is one of the most beautiful mosques of Agra. Anguri Bagh, Khaas Mahal and Jahangir Palace are must watch places. Every Evening you can come and enjoy the light show which showcases the Agra Fort's History. The entry fees are around 70 INR. You need to pay 40 INR to enter the Gate and for foreigners it is 550 INR. It is open from 6AM-6PM. 3. Fatehpur Sikri Fort. You can see the Fort behind me. Fatehpur and Sikri are 2 different areas, Sikri is home to a Fort where as Fathepur is known for its Mosque. This place has a deep historic value to Mughal Emperor Akbar. Akbar used to stay here with his 3 wives. When Akbar did not conceive any child from first 2 wives, he came to this Dargah and wished for a child, later Jahangir was born to Jodha and Akbar. Sikri Fort's History involves stories of his 3 wives. They had separate rooms and even separate Kitchens, Veg and Non Veg both. After coming here you will find out, why it is so famous. It is just 1 hour drive from Agra. Your Agra Trip will be incomplete, without visiting this place. Akbar created this place between 1552-1585. When Akbar did not conceive any child from first 2 wives, he came to this Dargah A Sufi Saint, Salim Chishti suggested Akbar to marry Jodha. Jahangir was also known as Salim, in the memory of Salim Chishti. Akbar also named Sikri as Shukhri As the locals were known by this name. The locals had helped him to win the 1527 battle, thus he paid them tribute by naming the city Sikri. There is no entry fees to visit the Mosque, but to visit the Sikri Fort, the entry fees is 40 INR for Indians and 550 INR for Foreigners. You may need to pay 30 INR extra for bus tickets till the Fort. It is open from 9AM-6PM. 4. Itimad-Ud-Daulah This place is also known as Mini Taj. Itimad-Ud-Daulah is a smaller version of Taj Mahal. Itimad-Ud-Daulah was the Treasurer of Mughals and Father of Noor Jahan. He was so dedicated that Jahangir gave him this title and later married his Daughter. Noor Jahan made this place from 1622 to 1628. If you are coming to visit Taj Mahal, do visit this place. Itimad-Ud-Daulah is known as Mini Taj Mahal. This place is dedicated to Mirza Baig, father-in-law of Jahangir. Noor Jahan made this place in the memory of her father who was the most dedicated Treasurer of Mughal Empire. Itimad-Ud-Daulah is made in Persian Architecture and is also known as Baby Taj. This place offers great historic insights that one must not miss. The entry fee for Indians is 5 INR and Foreigners- 210 INR. It is open from 8AM-12 AM 5. Mariyam's Tomb. I am at the Tomb of Mariyam. Mariyam was Akbar's 3rd wife and the mother of Jahangir. Mariyam was the second name of Jodha Bai. Jahangir made this place in the loving memory of her mother. Akbar's 3rd wife was also known as Mariyam-uz-Zamani Begum. This place is also known as Hira Kumari. It is located near the crematorium of Akbar and was made in 1623 by Jahangir. This place is made in Mughal Architecture, you can see Mughal Gardens all around. The Tomb is shaped like Lotus and is among the Top attractions of Agra. The entry is free and this place is open from 6AM-5PM. 6. Akbar's Tomb Behind this gate you can see Akbar's Makbara The Tomb of Greatest Mughal Emperor is located near to Agra. It is located just in front of the Tomb of Mariyam. One of the most unique fact about this place is, Akbar started it's construction and passed away in half, later this building was constructed by his son, Jahangir. It is definitely one of the Top attractions of Agra. One cannot miss visiting the Tomb of Akbar. Akbar was also known as the Sikandar. This Tomb involves both Hindu and Islam Architecture. This Tomb is an example of how powerful Mughals were. The entry fees are 15 INR for Indians and 110 INR for forigners. Timings : 6AM-6PM. 7. Mehtab Bagh I am at the Mehtab Park. Other than Taj Mahal, Shah Jahan created 11 parks and the last one was Mehtab Bagh, From this place, you get to see a beautiful view of the Taj Mahal. Mehtab Bagh is separated from Taj Mahal by Yamuna River. Shah Jahan wanted to make another Taj Mahal of Black colour at this place. He was not able to succeed as his son, Aurangzeb put him in life imprisonment. Mehtab Bagh was the place where the construction of Black Taj Mahal was about to begin. Yamuna River flows between Mehtab Bagh and Taj Mahal. This Mehtab Bagh is spread across 300 Acres and is a great tourist attraction. The best time to visit this garden is during the Sunset. Here you will see beautiful fountains, joggers park, pavilion and many exotic plants. This place is very popular among photographers. Entry ticketsl: 25 INR for Indians and 300 INR for foreigners. It is open from 6AM-6.30 PM You can pay extra 15 INR to get close to the Taj Mahal. 8. Chini ka Rauza. The next place to explore in Agra is Chini ka Rauza. This place is dedicated to the President of Shah Jahan kingdom; Afzal Khan It was made in 1635, and back then it was decorated with blue stones. The beauty of this place was unmatchable. Currently this monument is under maintenance. You can spot a beautiful park and a great Yamuna River scene. The place is pretty isolated but can be reached using any commercial vehicle. If you are coming from a 4 wheeler, the parking is far from this building. This Persian Architecture made up of blue stones is known as Chini ka Rauza. It was dedicated to the Wazir Afzal Khan Allami. It was made in 1635 in Ittmatpur. China Clay was used to make its tiles. This Blue Tomb was way beautiful back then. Though it is a Persian Architecture but the construction of Tomb resembles to Afghani Architecture. The entry is free and this place is open from 6AM- 6PM. 9. Ram Bagh This place was made by Babar and was renovated by Noor Jahan, wife of Jahangir. It is one of the most beautiful and old garden of Agra. Earlier, it was known as Araam Bagh You will find many Islamic Architecture buildings. Ram Bagh is a must-visit place for all History and Nature lovers. This place has no fees and is open throughout the day. 10. Keetham lake This place is also known as Soor Sarovar. It is named after Soordas and is also known as Keetham Lake. This place is a Bird Sanctuary and it is so big that you need a vehicle to explore it. You will spot a beautiful Keetham lake and a Bear Park. You will spot around 24-25 Sloth Bears, The ticket will be available on the entry gate Entry ticket is 30 INR and Bear Park ticket is 20 INR. If you are bringing your vehicle it will cost you 100 INR extra. Soor Sarovar is a perfect place for all Nature Lovers. Soor Sarovar, popularly known as Keetham Lake, This place is located just 20-25 mins away from Agra. This place is a home to Migratory Birds and some Big Mamals, There's a park dedicated to Sloth Bears. You can also go boating, and is a must-visit place for photography lovers. The best time to visit is during the Sunset. Do come here with your Family and Friends. Entry ticket is 30 INR and Bear Park ticket is 20 INR. Vehicle charges- 100 INR. It is open from 8AM-6PM. 11. Jama Masjid. I am at Agra's Jama Masjid. This place was made in the memory of Jahanara Begum. It is not possible to reach this place by a vehicle. Do come here by walk or rent a 2-wheeler. It is made up of Red Sandstone with carvings of White Marble. The art inside the building reflects the love of a dad for his daughter. This Mosque was made in the 17th Century. It is among the top Heritage tourist attractions of Agra. It is also known as Jumme wala Masjid. Every Friday, thousands of devotees come here to offer their prayers. This structure is made up of red sandstone with carvings made with white marble. The 12 Tombs add extra beauty to this Mosque. The entry is free and the timings are 7AM-6.30 PM. 12. Anguri Bagh This Garden was created by Shah Jahan in 1637. Back then grapes were cultivated in this Garden. This place is inside the Agra Fort, we have mentioned it especially because, this place has its own importance, This place was used by the Queens to spend their leisure time. It is covered with red sandstone building from 3 sides and Khaas Mahal on one side. This place had 85 symmetrical gardens were grapes were cultivated. This place is a must visit location. Anguri Bagh was made specially for the Mughal Queens, for them to spend their leisure time. In between of Khaas Mahal and Anguri Bagh you will find a beautiful fountain. It was made in 1637 by Shah Jahan and is covered with lush and beautiful gardens. You will also find a Turkish Bathtub also known as Hammam. It is located in the Agra Fort, the entry fees is 40 INR for Indians and 550 INR for foreigners. It is open from 6AM-6PM. 13. Guru ka Taal Gurudwara. It is among the top spiritual attractions of Agra. It is the most popular Gurudwara of Agra. It is dedicated to Guru Tegh Bahadur who is the 9th Guru of Sikhs. He sacrificed his life in front of Aurangzeb, as he refused to convert himself to Islam. This place used to be a water reservoir under teh rule of Jahangir. Later, Gurudwara was constructed here. It is open throughout the day and has no entry fees. 14. Shree Mankameshwar Temple. This Temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is among the largest Shiva Temple of Agra. The Shivalinga here dates back to dwaparyuga and is covered with Silver. After the birth of Lord Krsna, Lord Shiva wanted to meet and greet him so he came as a Saint to this place and meditated for 1 day to meet Lord Krsna, Yashoda Maiya denied Lord Shiva to meet Lord Krsna In the end he somehow managed to sight Lord Krsna. If you are fond of spiritual places do come to Mankameshwar Temple. There are thousands of devotees who visit this Temple daily. During Shravan festival, many devotees come here. It is one of the biggest Shiva Temple of Uttar Pradesh. It is said that the Shivalinga here dates back to Dvaparyuga. It is open from 5AM-10PM and the entry is free of cost. 15. Agra Sadar Bazar. One must not miss the Sadar Bazaar of Agra. It is similar to CP or Chandni Chowk of Delhi. Here you can buy all type of Artifacts, garments, shoes or if you are fond of food then a street is dedicated to fast food. You can come here for dinner or to have snacks. One must definitely visit the Sadar Bazaar. Sadar Bazaar is among the biggest and most popular markets of Agra It is located beside the Agra Railway Station. Here you can come here to buy handicrafts, artifacts, accessories, etc. You can come here to have both veg and non-veg food. The famous Chaat Gully is located in this market. The famous Panchi Petha is located here. If you love shopping then do come here. The entry is free and this place is open throughout the day. Click on the Google Maps link in the description to access all the locations. So, these were the top places to visit in Agra. I hope you liked the video, press the like button and also subscribe to the channel, if you have more doubts, check the complete travel guide of Agra video, From food, itinerary, transportation, hotels, etc. everything is covered in this video. The link is in the description. If you want all this information easily accessible. Click the link in the description to download the Anuj Bucket App. We have properly organised and summarised all the details of your Agra Trip. Download the App to enjoy exciting content. Be well, Namaste. You will find all the behind-the-scenes of our trip on our Instagram page Follow @anujbucket. Stay tuned for more amazing content.

🔵About Agra -
Agra is a city offering a discovery of the beautiful era. Agra has a rich history, reflected in its numerous monuments dotted in and around the city. The earliest citation for Agra comes from the mythological era, where the epic Mahabharata refer Agra as  Agravana meaning paradise in Sanskrit. Ptolemy the famous second century A.D. geographer, was the first person who referred Agra with its modern name. The Modern Agra was founded by Sikandar Lodi, ruler of Lodi dynasty in 16th century. It was when Shah Jahan descended the Mughal throne that Agra reached the zenith of architectural beauty.

The city lies in the Western part of Uttar Pradesh on the bank of River Yamuna. Though the wonderful allure of the Taj Mahal attracts people from around the world over to Agra, it is not a standalone attraction. The city offers a trail of fascinating tombs and mausoleums to explore. Acclaimed for its lavish crafts like Pietra Dura/ marble inlay work, rugs and leather goods, and the luscious Petha, Agra equally caters well to shopaholics and foodies.
